content: With the current Bindgen implementation an non const default trait, every change to VTABLES gives us a non building rust crate. Maybe #[derive(Default)] can be implemented in a cost way, or Bindgen can add a const default definition, so we can do ..Self::default() or similar in the VTABLE
Const trait impls are not (yet) possible. An inherent method should work fine.
Mainly this is something I would wish for rust-for-linux, or somebody to tell me how I can fix this at this time.
An inherent method is a method in an impl Type {} block as opposed to an impl Trait for Type {} block.
So basically impl SomeType { const fn default() -> Self { ... } } is fine, but `impl Default for SomeType { const fn default() -> Self { ... } } is not.

Yes, But We do not own the type, So I guess Rust will just say NO.
Also this has to be auto generated somehow, So that a new field to the ops struct automatically gets a new default created.

It is bindgen generated, right? These autogenerated definitions are part of the kernel crate, so you can add this method anywhere in the kernel crate. As for autogeneration I expected that bindgen would allow deriving arbitrary traits (eg some ConstDefault derive macro that adds an inherent const fn default), but it seems not. Maybe it would be possible to add this option to bindgen?
